title: Big-Budget Campaigns. Without the Big Budget. In Masonry.
meta: Vertical 9:16 reel, ~25s. Featured brand: Cuyana. Inspired by DU_GQB9iAph. Voiceover-led: a confident creator-founder VO carries the reel; on-screen karaoke supers echo single words on the beat. Full editor brief: VO, supers, visual, the exact Masonry UI element to show per beat, edit, transitions, SFX.
concept: A confident small-brand empowerment reel that mirrors the inspiration beat for beat: a model in a pink suit with a Cuyana top-handle bag, a Paris campaign, surreal big-scale moments (a giant bag towed through the street, the brand's own posters on city walls). The pitch is that any small brand can produce big-budget-looking campaigns now, because Masonry generates them from the brand's real product, brand look, and model faces. The key edit note throughout: wherever the original shows a finished campaign shot, a billboard, or the giant-bag set piece, we show the Masonry interface generating it from the real Cuyana bag and the brand's own model, so the big-budget look is literally produced in Masonry, with no shoot in Paris.
audio: Voiceover reel. A confident, encouraging creator-founder VO carries the whole story; record it dry and close. On-screen supers are karaoke-style: single key words from the VO popping on the beat, clean bold sans plus a script accent for the good news line. Stylish, upbeat track underneath; light whooshes on the big-scale reveals, soft UI ticks on the Masonry-screen beats.
masonry_about: Masonry is an AI creative studio for brands. You upload three things, your product, your brand look, and your model faces, and it generates full campaign visuals, photo and video, from them: location campaigns, billboards, surreal big-scale set pieces, all keeping the exact product and the exact faces. THE WHOLE POINT OF THIS EDIT: the inspiration reel keeps cutting to finished campaign shots in Paris, billboard posters, and a giant-bag set piece. In our version, do not just show a beautiful finished campaign image. Show the Masonry UI element making it: the real Cuyana bag uploaded onto the Masonry canvas, the brand's model face added as a reference, the Paris campaign frame generating in the result panel, the billboard and the giant-bag scene rendered in Masonry. The Masonry screen replaces the finished campaign shot. That substitution is the proof of the line: big-budget campaigns, made in Masonry, without the big budget.
refsummary: The original is a polished small-brand empowerment reel. A model in a pink suit carries a fuzzy green top-handle bag through Paris; single words land on the beat: Stop, playing, deserve, big, stage, you, the good news, to create big campaigns, thanks to AI, accessible, your products, your brand, your faces, big campaigns, big budgets, without, then comment AI to learn how. It uses surreal big-scale visuals (a giant version of the bag towed on a bike cart, the brand's posters on city walls) and ends on a tight beauty shot over the green bag. The single words are karaoke captions of the spoken voiceover. Watch it once for the confident pacing and the product-brand-faces trio. Our version rebuilds it with Cuyana as the brand and Masonry as the AI doing the work.
story: A you-can-do-this-now arc, voiceover-led. HOOK: stop playing small, just because you are a small brand does not mean you do not deserve the big stage. GOOD NEWS: now you can create big campaigns, thanks to AI, and it is finally accessible. HOW: drop in your products, your brand look, and your faces, and Masonry produces the campaign. PROOF: a full Paris campaign, billboards, big-scale set pieces, all generated, all on brand, all with the real bag and the real model. PAYOFF: big-budget production value, without the big budget. CTA: comment AI to learn how. The viewer should believe a small brand can make campaigns that look like a major label's, using Masonry, from its own product and faces.
takeaway: A small brand can produce big-budget-looking campaigns, location shoots, billboards, surreal big-scale visuals, by giving Masonry its product, its brand look, and its model faces. Masonry generates the campaign; the brand skips the budget.
script_kind: Voiceover script. This reel is VO-led: the lines below are spoken, word for word, by a confident creator-founder voice. The on-screen supers are karaoke captions, single key words from each line popping on the beat (each beat's Super field shows which words). Read top to bottom for the complete spoken script.

script.line: Just because you are a small brand,
does not mean you do not deserve the big stage. You do.
And here is the good news.
You can create big campaigns now, thanks to AI.
Your products. Your brand. Your faces.
In big campaigns that look like a major label shot them.
Big-budget production. Without the big budget.

beats.super: Karaoke: Stop slams, then playing, bold white.
beats.visual: Tight, confident frame of the model in the pink suit, direct to camera, the Cuyana bag in hand.
beats.masonry: Generate this opener in Masonry from the real Cuyana bag and the brand's model face: confident pink-suit portrait, the bag held, the face locked. Show it as a Masonry generation, not a captured photo.
beats.transition: Cut in on the first word.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 0 to 1: the model close-up with Stop and playing.

beats.vo: Just because you are a small brand,
beats.super: Karaoke: just because you are, then doesn't mean holds.
beats.visual: The surreal big-scale moment: a giant version of the Cuyana bag towed on a bike cart down a Paris street, playful and impossible.
beats.masonry: Generate the giant-bag set piece in Masonry from the real bag: prompt a giant version of the Cuyana bag on a cart in a Paris street, the bag identical to the product. Show it as a Masonry render, the kind of scene a real shoot could never stage cheaply.
beats.edit: Wide hold on the absurd scale.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 2 to 4: the street and bike frames and the giant bag on the cart with doesn't mean and just because you are.

beats.vo: does not mean you do not deserve
beats.visual: Cut back to the model, assured, the bag prominent.
beats.masonry: Generate in Masonry from the Cuyana bag and the model face: a strong portrait beat, on brand, holding the line.

beats.super: Karaoke: big oversized, then stage, then you punches.
beats.visual: The model holds the green Cuyana bag up like a trophy, then a cut riding through the street with it, aspirational and bold.
beats.masonry: Generate both frames in Masonry from the same bag and face: the held-up hero and the street ride, product and face unchanged. Masonry renders, not a location shoot.
beats.edit: Two cuts on big and you.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 6 to 8: the bag held up and the ride with big, stage, you.

beats.vo: And here is the good news.
beats.super: the good news in a warm script accent, slightly different from the bold supers.
beats.visual: A softer, brighter frame of the bag, a pivot point into the how.
beats.masonry: Generate in Masonry from the bag: a clean, bright product-forward frame to mark the turn.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 9: the good news script frame.

beats.vo: You can create big campaigns now, thanks to AI.
beats.super: Karaoke: to create, then big campaigns, then thanks to AI.
beats.visual: The brand's own campaign posters on Paris walls (one reads as big as you), with the giant bag in the street below: the campaign is live, citywide.
beats.masonry: This is the central Masonry beat. Show the billboards and the campaign being generated in Masonry from the Cuyana bag and the brand's model: the poster art produced in the result panel, then composited onto the city wall. Replace the finished billboards with the Masonry screen that made them, the AI in thanks to AI is Masonry.
beats.edit: Reveal the posters, then the giant bag below.
beats.transition: Cut from the Masonry render to the wall.
beats.sfx: Soft UI ticks, then a whoosh to the street.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 10 to 11: be, to create, and the billboards with to create big campaigns.

beats.visual: A clean hand-held frame of the green Cuyana bag, the product close and tactile.
beats.masonry: Generate in Masonry from the real bag: a tactile close product frame, proving the exact product carries through every generation.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 12 to 13: thanks to AI and accessible with the bag in hand.

beats.vo: Your products. Your brand. Your faces.
beats.ost: your products. your brand. your faces.
beats.super: Karaoke: your products, then your brand, then your faces, one per cut.
beats.visual: Three quick cuts: the bag held out (product), a styled on-brand frame (brand), a tight beauty close-up of the model (faces).
beats.masonry: Map this trio to the three Masonry inputs explicitly: your products is the Cuyana bag uploaded to Masonry, your brand is the brand look and palette set as the DNA, your faces is the model face added as a reference. Show each input going into Masonry, then the on-brand frame it generates. This beat teaches what the editor literally does in the tool.
beats.edit: Three cuts on the three words.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 14 to 17: your products, your brand, your faces.

beats.vo: In big campaigns that look like a major label shot them.
beats.super: Karaoke: big campaigns holds over the landmark.
beats.visual: A postcard campaign frame: the model with the Cuyana bag, the Eiffel Tower behind, the kind of location shot that usually costs a fortune.
beats.masonry: Generate the landmark campaign frame in Masonry from the bag and face: a Paris location look, the product and model unchanged. A Masonry render standing in for an expensive location shoot.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 18 to 19: the Eiffel Tower frames with big campaigns.

beats.vo: Big-budget production. Without the big budget.
beats.super: Karaoke: big budgets, then without struck in.
beats.visual: A punchy hero frame, the campaign at its most premium, the contradiction landing.
beats.masonry: Generate in Masonry from the bag and face: a premium hero frame, the proof that big-budget polish came out of Masonry, not a budget.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 20 to 21: big budgets and without.

beats.super: comment AI oversized, then to learn how; then the Cuyana x Masonry lockup.
beats.visual: Tight beauty close-up of the model resting over the green Cuyana bag, direct and warm, then a clean end card.
beats.masonry: Close on a final Masonry-generated beauty frame from the bag and face, then the Cuyana x Masonry end card.
beats.edit: Hold the close-up, cut to the end card.
beats.transition: Final cut, hold the end card.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 22 to 24: the smile and the comment AI to learn how CTA.

notes: Editor workflow: record the VO first and cut every karaoke super and scene change to its stresses; each super is single words from the VO line (see each beat's Super field). The single rule that makes this reel work: never show a finished campaign shot, a billboard, or the giant-bag set piece on its own. Always show the Masonry UI element generating it from the real Cuyana bag and the brand's own model face (see each beat's Make in Masonry line). The teaching beat is your products / your brand / your faces at 14 to 17s: show those three as the three things you actually drop into Masonry (product upload, brand DNA, face reference), then the on-brand frame Masonry returns. The central proof beat is the billboards at 10 to 13s: those posters should be generated in Masonry, which is the AI in thanks to AI. Load the real Cuyana bag and the model face into Masonry once and generate every beat from them so the product and face are identical throughout. Cuyana is a stand-in demo brand; swap in the real client when producing. No media generated yet: generation is gated on separate approval per project policy.
